_CUP OF JOE ON THE ISSUES_
# LIVESTREAM: "What is going on with Iran: national security and restoring U.S. global leadership"
## _Saturday, July 27th, Clive, Iowa_
**Today at 10.20am EDT** , I'll be kicking off the first of a series of our own events: "**Cup of Joe on the Issues,** " starting with a **conversation on Iran. **You can **watch the event on a Facebook live stream[here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8586560/83322676/1972450637?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NGNhMzM5MC03OWIwLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wjVB2M3-lFGawFRZGzC4C9ebkuLniSDxAs5wDJO7OsU=&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAyLS35WIL14jMf4D8x1gGvYK7QAL5bqONjONh14DtQ4Wp_fECC4THBXZw5Cvh2t41pQSA1wbNohbx1z_-tLDGdQRJO7zf11iqo1yXDscXYAUzPZjeIP02BX7qdcPLQIiFi2mVz4tA-liAi2kOuN4nqfpC7eqUsCYYoF_VHb2f3ZOKX3UYY9QgROPxCKI26GLDmgsVYWCc8acZmfHoMyBT4S5UpoUHROEqVgEuEO5u4_j0YQfzKLN7Oup4kpG_ATVWFnUn3zvO4_J4hZbWzWLtTitVCyOZra23IIz2dnkM7NZZkg6_vodSqKXCQ-CTFfV3AbJ8gWt0vBGg6A4an6Q&__tn__=-R) \- again, at 10.20am EDT today, Saturday, July 27th.**
This follows on from a recent op-ed I authored in the Des Moines Register ([link](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/8586561/83322677/654585162?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NGNhMzM5MC03OWIwLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wjVB2M3-lFGawFRZGzC4C9ebkuLniSDxAs5wDJO7OsU=)) where I wrote on the folly of potential conflict with Iran, and the need to return to the nuclear deal and honor our word. It'll also be a chance to talk more generally about foreign policy and national security issues. As President Bill Clinton's former Director for Defense Policy and a former 3-star Admiral who commanded an aircraft carrier battle group in the Arabian Gulf, I hope you'll find my insights useful.
Please share the event with your friends and family. I'll make this quick because I'm on the road, and I've got to get back to talking to voters.

I know that we're strong enough to take on Cotton's Washington network, but I need you to be all in for this fight. Can I count on you to chip in $5 today and help me hit my $50,000 July fundraising goal?

| | Hi Peter, tomorrow marks **100 days out from Election Day** , so I've spent the past few days reflecting on Matt Bevin's tenure as governor. Unfortunately, as a former governor and a proud Kentuckian, I can't find much to be proud of in Bevin's record.

His bullying of Kentucky's public servants and his attacks on our health care are tough to watch. I'm also troubled by his complete lack of transparency and accountability and his constant refusal to take responsibility for his actions. **Kentuckians deserve better.**

But there's a light at the end of this tunnel: **tomorrow we 'll be 100 days away from electing someone who knows the value of public service and is dedicated to putting Kentuckians first.**

That someone is my son, Andy Beshear. As governor, he'll defend health care for working families, support our teachers and public schools, create good jobs, and protect pensions.

**Can you donate right now to help Andy finish this campaign strong? The clock is ticking!** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

name: Jason Yates
subject: Critical Updates from the Religious Freedom Summit
body:
| | Peter, My Faith Votes was honored to have our Chairman Sealy Yates attend the second Ministerial to Advance Religious Freedom Summit in Washington D.C last week. We talked with Sealy to learn more about the event, the critical takeaways for Christians, and how we can speak out on behalf of the persecuted and the voiceless across the world. **[Read our full conversation here](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvMGnduLjK2lzpQvSrk4KqPLb6d2WHB1u2m_0ZqyI6iphCtEXslDXtBBs8RLL4EhUk1PhHtwxw0LhNQUyX55OoOu&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190727com01&n=1).** What was it like to be at such a historic summit with 130 different nations represented? Sealy: It was a gathering, unlike anything that I have ever experienced. The first thing that struck me is religious freedom means what it says. It means religious freedom for all. If we deny religious freedom to those who believe differently than us, even if we think they are wrong, then we have no right to complain if we are denied our religious freedom. I found it encouraging and refreshing to rub shoulders with people from a number of different faiths, and I was moved by hearing them speak about being persecuted for their faith in various parts of the world. One of the most interesting pieces of information from the summit is the powerful statistic that 80 percent of those who are persecuted for their faith in the world today are Christians. And when you understand scripture, you understand why. Because it is Christ in you that is being persecuted, not you. If you don’t have Christ, then it is for other reasons that you are being persecuted. Read the **[full interview for the rest of Sealy’s take-aways](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvMGnduLjK2lzpQvSrk4KqPLb6d2WHB1u2m_0ZqyI6iphCtEXslDXtBBs8RLL4EhUk1PhHtwxw0LhNQUyX55OoOu&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190727com01&n=2)** from the Ministerial to Advance Religious Freedom. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvMGnduLjK2lzpQvSrk4KqPLb6d2WHB1u2m_0ZqyI6iphCtEXslDXtBBs8RLL4EhUk1PhHtwxw0LhNQUyX55OoOu&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190727com01&n=3) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190727com01&n=4)

I am working every day to keep that promise, but I need to know I have your support. My team has challenged me to raise $25,000 before the end of the month, and that is what we are going to do. Our opponents raise money from coastal elites and giant tech executives, we’re looking to raise money from hardworking Americans tired of being ignored. It all begins with you. Elizabeth has a plan to:
* Cancel student loan debt and create universal free public college
* Lead the global effort against climate change
* Make high-quality child care affordable for all
* Make sure that no president is above the law
* Create a fair and welcoming immigration system
* Solve the affordable housing crisis
* Pass federal laws that protect the constitutional rights in Roe v. Wade
* Replace the Electoral College with a national popular vote
* Ensure that corporate executives face jail time for overseeing massive scams
* Make sure the biggest corporations pay their fair share
* Protect public lands
* Break up big tech
* Improve our military housing
* Address the black maternal mortality crisis
* Provide debt relief for Puerto Rico
* End the opioid crisis
* Reduce corporate influence at the Pentagon
* Put American wages ahead of Wall Street bonuses
* Close the startup capital gap for entrepreneurs of color
* End private prisons and stop companies from exploiting those in our criminal and immigration systems
* Secure our elections and stop racist voter suppression
* Rebuild the State Department
* Level the playing field for America's family farmers
* Promote equity in the private sector and boost wages for women of color
* Accelerate the transition to clean energy
* End Wall Street's stranglehold on our economy
* Stop the next economic crash
* ...and more

Big, structural change for our country requires bold ideas. It also requires a massive grassroots movement fighting hard to make it happen. And we can't get it done if we miss any monthly fundraising goals.

| **_REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [The Do-Over Delusion](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0ea7647914&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Washington Times_
“Beating a dead horse is an exercise in futility. Hammering on Robert Mueller, author of the vaunted Mueller Report, has proved no more productive,” _The Washington Times_ editorial board writes. “No matter how many times Democrats demand a do-over, the results won’t change . . . Each copy [of the report] spells out in black and white the failure to find that Mr. Trump committed a crime.”

[While Democrats wasted 2 years, President Trump delivered REAL results](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4bcca525db&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

| [Death of the Impeachment Dream](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=47544682a4&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Wall Street Journal_
“House Democrats hoped that Wednesday’s hearings would allow them to highlight their favorite portions of the Mueller report for people who haven’t read it. Democrats didn’t expect this segment of Americans to include the author,” James Freeman writes. Regarding “a document with which he seemed strangely unfamiliar, former Special Counsel Robert Mueller let stand the report’s finding of no Trump-Russia collusion.”

**Rep. John Ratcliffe:** [The bedrock of justice is presumption of innocence](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fa7b0943d8&e=3ee4985eea)

-_The Epoch Times_
On Thursday, company executives and workers joined President Trump at the White House “to celebrate the one-year anniversary of the ‘Pledge to America’s Workers,’ an initiative to address the workforce skills shortage problem in the country,” Emel Akan reports. “The president’s call to action for the pledge has become a full-blown national movement,” Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump said.

**🎬** **Ivanka Trump:** [This Pledge means 12 million American lives improved](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=393f4368be&e=3ee4985eea)

-_New York Post_
“President Trump took part in the swearing-in ceremony for new Defense Secretary Mark Esper on Thursday — hailing his experience in the military and defense industry,” Bob Fredericks reports. “I have absolute confidence that Mark will ensure that our incredible military is fully prepared to deter conflict and defeat any foe,” the President said at the Pentagon. Esper was confirmed by the Senate on Tuesday by a 90-8 vote.

-Gallup
Jeffrey Jones writes that “after hitting a new high last month, mentions of immigration as the most important problem facing the U.S. increased further to 27% in July.” As Jones explains, that number is historically significant. “Gallup has asked the ‘most important problem’ question since 1939 . . . Over the past 19 years, only five other issues have been mentioned at some point by at least the 27% who named immigration this month.”

Friend,
This has been an incredible month.

Two weeks ago, I spoke to a crowd of Hoosiers from all 92 counties in Indiana at the Hoosier Gym in Knightstown to officially announce my re-election campaign to serve as your Governor for another four years.

_The love and support I received from Hoosiers that day was gratifying and inspiring, but ultimately it wasn’t about me._

**It was about you.**

My time in office as Governor has always been about Putting People First, and my re-election campaign will be no different.
[ **Chip in any amount by 11:59 p.m. on July 31st** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=ebc12af399b74d07a69b21c73125bece&_e=c0YXKaH1KPIWEttDfV3h-fQznSsj-nZRH9xxnLpkLLVSTXmums2oLfXZUgLoNtESHAGAuMnLgEQi4dvn6L1KETOc-ehLy-QMOGxg_oiTOTTNW6eCkCn9iyPZNpScJ4PrCE45TiXS0pTRrY8xJcnADzi0-ehWAnvik7qyLWR4IqlfvHuo03wvnyb8yQ_0-kHC4S0JB1lLS_sxC8kFTSbAxPUPk2JhhLFy3glCy7Ez-1QTSm45BfnLRIPzLQhRTZQRgrg9pfCq-1otNtchZ1oLgFv5CcC8mZSt_Pn6UqjTRN-Nzeo3hbyV3X0udJ62mkzaGS7ZInYdwUnZ7QJG2oHDvKJODu9xM2B0XRH0dmhJRJQVbXSOWF63JXRUc1KCt97vBujKcR1CORVl7gsXyhhWwNVpIt08hztjvE0TxjHKdno%3D)
We’re Putting People First by making Indiana the best place to find a job and start a career.
We’re Putting People First by creating an economic ecosystem that today ranks BEST in the Midwest.
We’re breaking new job commitment records year-after-year. We’ve tripled foreign direct investment here on Hoosier soil while maintaining our AAA credit rating. And we now have more Hoosiers working in the private sector today than at any other time in our state’s history.
Jobs are up and our unemployment is down. Our wages are up, our revenues are up, our exports are up and our GDP is up.

Two weeks ago, I spoke to a crowd of Hoosiers from all 92 counties in Indiana at the Hoosier Gym in Knightstown to officially announce my re-election campaign to serve as your Governor for another four years. The love and support I received from Hoosiers that day was gratifying and inspiring, but ultimately it wasn’t about me. It was about you. My time in office as Governor has always been about Putting People First, and my re-election campaign will be no different. We’re Putting People First by making Indiana the best place to find a job and start a career. We’re Putting People First by creating an economic ecosystem that today ranks BEST in the Midwest. We’re breaking new job commitment records year-after-year. We’ve tripled foreign direct investment here on Hoosier soil while maintaining our AAA credit rating. And we now have more Hoosiers working in the private sector today than at any other time in our state’s history. Jobs are up and our unemployment is down. Our wages are up, our revenues are up, our exports are up and our GDP is up. name: Dan Baer
subject: I'm asking you personally
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
Low dollar grassroots donations help me fight back when Mitch McConnell, Trump and The Republican Party and the Koch brothers launch nasty attacks.
|
---

| | I won't sugar-coat it:

[**I need you to chip in $19 or whatever you can before my first fundraising deadline of the quarter. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827a7/232dfd1/9bc4136/62a12a41/888205810/VEsH/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190727_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")

When you hear about grassroots fundraising goals, you might be wondering if that's a made-up tactic.

Let me tell you: It's not.

Low-dollar grassroots donations help fund every ad I run.

They put gas in our pickup so we can travel around Colorado meeting voters.

They allow me to fight back when Mitch McConnell, Trump, The Republican Party, and the Koch brothers launch nasty attacks.

And when my numbers go public at the end of each quarter, political elites use grassroots numbers to size up the strength of our historic campaign.

That's why this matters.

And frankly, that's why we're going to be emailing you a lot this week --**_because grassroots donations like yours truly make the difference._**

[**I 'm personally reaching out before our big End of Month fundraising push to see if you can chip in a few bucks today to help us on our way. It makes more of a difference than you know. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827a7/232dfd1/9bc4136/62a12a41/888205810/VEsE/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190727_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")

I'm so grateful for everyone that has chipped in so far, large or small.

There's a lot of you who have proven just how much we all care about what's at stake.

Thanks,

Dan
|
---
[](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827a7/232dfd1/9bc4136/62a12a46/888205810/VEsF/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://www.facebook.com/DanForColorado/") [](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827a7/232dfd1/9bc4136/62a12a47/888205810/VEsC/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://twitter.com/danbbaer")

Colorado is the most important Senate race in the entire country according to Nate Silver and CNN. It's our only chance of flipping the Senate. And to win the most important Senate race, we need the most qualified candidate.

Dan served as an Ambassador under President Obama and as the head of the Colorado Department of Higher Education in Governor Hickenlooper's cabinet. If elected, he would be the first openly gay man to serve in Senate history.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but it's because we're running a grassroots campaign and rejecting corporate PAC money. We need your help to win -- and grassroots donations like yours truly make the difference. | | [**DONATE**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827a7/232dfd1/9bc4136/62a12a44/888205810/VEsD/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=footer&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")
---

We hope you'll consider sticking with us long-term. But if you'd like to unsubscribe, please [click here](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb827a7/232dfd1/9bc4136/62a12a4a/888205810/VEsB/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://go.danforcolorado.com/unsubscribe").

#
We’re still a bit behind on our fundraising goal for the month. One of the reasons it’s getting harder and harder to hit goals is that our average contribution has gone down quite a bit since we first started this campaign.
**This month our average donation is just $22.92.** If we want to make our July budget, we’re going to need a lot more of those donations.
The good news is that if even 1-in-5 of the people reading this email gave us another $22.92, we’d hit our monthly goal in just a few hours.
[Can you be one of the ones to chip in now? Whether you give $5, $10, or $22.92, your contribution makes a big difference as we stretch for our goal.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/21013?refcode=ema20190727-45297&t=19&akid=45297%2E444211%2EfhcQwP)

Thank you for always stepping up when we need you.
\- Team Beto

One of the reasons it’s getting harder and harder to hit goals is that our average contribution has gone down quite a bit since we first started this campaign. This month our average donation is just $22.92. If we want to make our July budget, we’re going to need a lot more of those donations. The good news is that if even 1-in-5 of the people reading this email gave us another $22.92, we’d hit our monthly goal in just a few hours. At the end of the day, an average donation of just $22.92 is something to be proud of. It shows that this is a campaign that is truly funded by people — not PACs, lobbyists, or special interests. Thank you for always stepping up when we need you. \- Team Beto Paid for by Beto for America

| Friend,

In just over 72 hours, John will take the stage alongside some of the biggest names in the presidential race, including three U.S. senators, three current and former congressional representatives, a fellow governor, a mayor and a bestselling author.

But even on a stage full of well-known people with big ideas, we're confident that John will stand out. John has more than just ideas--**he has experience and real plans to move our country forward.**

That's what makes John the right person for this job. From passing comprehensive gun safety legislation in a purple state to making long-acting reversible birth control accessible to all women in Colorado, John is the only candidate in this race with a proven track record of accomplishing progressive goals.

We know John is a strong candidate, and he's gained traction in recent polls--but we're running out of time to grow this support. This debate is a turning point, and once John walks off stage we'll really be in the thick of primary season.

This race is only getting more competitive--and we need your help to make sure John is a frontrunner. [**_Even $1 can ensure make John 's message is heard.
